elements.sent-2411-001: We present a nearly linear work parallel algorithm for approximating the Held--Karp bound for \mTSP.
elements.sent-2411-002: Given an edge-weighted undirected graph $G=(V,E)$ on $m$ edges and $\varepsilon>0$, it returns a $(1+\varepsilon)$-approximation to the Held--Karp bound with high probability, in $\tilde{O (m/\varepsilon^4)$ work\footnote{The soft Oh notation $\tilde O (\cdot)$ hides polylogarithmic factors in $m $ and $\varepsilon^{-1}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-003: While a nearly linear time sequential algorithm was known for almost a decade (Chekuri and Quanrud~'17), it was not known how to simultaneously achieve nearly linear work alongside polylogarithmic depth.
elements.sent-2411-004: Using a reduction by Chalermsook et al.~'22, we also give a parallel algorithm for computing a $(1+\varepsilon)$-approximate fractional solution to the $k$-edge-connected spanning subgraph (\kECSS) problem, with the same complexity.

elements.sent-2411-006: For \mTSP~and \kECSS, core-sequences enable us to exploit the structure of approximate minimum cuts to reduce the cost per iteration and/or the number of iterations.

elements.sent-2411-010: The Traveling Salesman Problem (\TSP) is among the most well studied problems in combinatorial optimization and theoretical computer science, constituting almost an entire field of research on its own \cite{LawlerLRS91, ApplegateBCC06, GuntinP07, Cook12 .}
elements.sent-2411-011: The input to \TSP~is a graph $G=(V, E)$ with positive edge costs $c\in \R^E_{>0}$, and the goal is to find a minimum cost Hamiltonian cycle in $G$.
elements.sent-2411-012: It is well known that the problem is inapproximable already on undirected graphs, by a reduction from the Hamiltonian cycle problem.
elements.sent-2411-013: This impossibility result, as well as many practical applications, motivated the study of \mTSP.
elements.sent-2411-014: Given $(G,c)$, the goal now is to find a minimum-cost tour in $G$, where a tour is a closed walk which visits all the vertices.
elements.sent-2411-015: So, every vertex can be visited multiple times.

elements.paragraph-2411-001: Importance of solving the Subtour Elimination LP

elements.sent-2411-037: A problem closely related to \mTSP~is the k-edge-connected spanning multi-subgraph problem (\kECSM).
elements.sent-2411-038: In \kECSM, given an undirected graph $G=(V,E)$ with positive edge costs $c\in \R^E_{>0}$ and an integer $k\geq 1$, the goal is to find a minimum-cost $k$-edge-connected multi-subgraph of $G$ which spans $V$.
elements.sent-2411-039: A multi-subgraph of $G$ is a subgraph of $G$ with the exception that every edge can be taken multiple times, but every copy needs to be payed for.
elements.sent-2411-040: The canonical LP relaxation for \kECSM~is the following Cut Covering LP: \begin{equation}\label{lp:cut_cover} \begin{aligned} \operatorname{CC}(G,c,k) = \min\; c^\top y \text { s.t. } \sum_{e\in \delta(S)} y_e\geq k \qquad \forall\, \emptyset \subsetneq S \subsetneq V \qquad\; y_e \geq 0 \qquad\qquad\;\; \forall\, e\in E. \end{aligned}\end{equation} Clearly, $\operatorname{CC (G,c,k) = k\operatorname{CC (G,c,1)$ for all $k\geq 0$.}}
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}\label{lp:cut_cover}  \operatorname{CC}(G,c,k) = \min\; c^\top y \text { s.t. } \sum_{e\in \delta(S)} y_e\geq k \qquad \forall\, \emptyset \subsetneq S \subsetneq V \qquad\; y_e \geq 0 \qquad\qquad\;\; \forall\, e\in E. \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-041: Cunningham~\cite{MonmaMP90 and Goemans and Bertsimas~\cite{GoemansB93 showed that for any graph $G$ with edge costs $c\in \R^E_{>0}$, the optimal value of the Subtour Elimination LP \eqref{def_SE_LP for the metric completion $(\hat G, \hat c)$ coincides with the optimal value of \eqref{lp:cut_cover for $(G,c)$ with $k=2$, i.e., $\operatorname{SE (\hat G, \hat c) = \operatorname{CC (G,c,2)$.}}}}}}

elements.subsection-2411-001: The MWU Framework for Packing/Covering LPs
elements.sent-2411-065: Since our algorithm follows the MWU approach, we now provide a brief overview of this framework and our innovation within it.
elements.sent-2411-066: Given a nonnegative matrix $A\in \R^{m\times N _{\geq 0}$, a packing LP is of the form \begin{equation}\label{lp:pack} \max_{x\geq \0} \{\ang{\1,x}:Ax \leq \1\}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-067: Its dual is a covering LP \begin{equation}\label{lp:cover} \min_{y\geq \0} \{\ang{\1,y}:A^{\top}y \geq \1\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-068: Clearly, \eqref{lp:cut_cover can be converted into \eqref{lp:cover by scaling the rows and columns.}}

elements.sent-2411-071: Given $\varepsilon>0$, the algorithm of~\cite{PST95 computes a $(1+\varepsilon)$-approximate solution to \eqref{lp:pack by iteratively calling a linear minimization oracle on a weighted average of the constraints.}}
elements.sent-2411-072: Given a convex domain $\mathcal{D}$ and weights $w^{(t)}\in \R^m_{\geq 0}$, the oracle returns $g^{(t) := \argmin_{x\in \mathcal{D \langle A^\top w^{(t) , x\rangle$.}}}}

elements.sent-2411-074: Unfortunately, the updates in \cite{PST95 can be very slow, as they need to be scaled by $1/\rho$, where $\rho:= \max_t \|Ag^{(t)}\|_\infty$ is the width of the oracle.}
elements.sent-2411-075: This drawback was overcome in the subsequent influential work of Garg and Könemann~\cite{journals/siamcomp/GargK07 , who gave the first width-independent MWU algorithm for packing/covering LPs.}
elements.sent-2411-076: Their algorithm requires a similar oracle, which solves the following subproblem in every iteration $t$: Given weights $w^{(t)}\in \R^m_{\geq 0}$, find a minimum weight column of $A$ \begin{equation}\label{sys:gk_sub} j^*\in \argmin_{j\in [N]} (A^\top w^{(t)})_j.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-077: The main innovation of \cite{journals/siamcomp/GargK07 was to adaptively scale the oracle response in order to achieve width independence.}
elements.sent-2411-078: Specifically, they set $g^{(t) := \alpha e_{j^*}$ where $\alpha$ is chosen such that $\|Ag^{(t)}\|_\infty = 1$.}

elements.paragraph-2411-008: Clearing an Epoch Using a Core-Sequence
elements.sent-2411-114: We present a general framework for clearing an epoch, by introducing the notion of core-sequence.
elements.sent-2411-115: The basic idea is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-116: In every iteration $t$, instead of updating all the coordinates in $B^{(t)}$ as in~\eqref{eq:young_update , we only update a fixed subset $\tilde B_1\subseteq B^{(t)}$.}
elements.sent-2411-117: In particular, we keep updating the variables in $ \tilde B_1\cap B^{(t)}$ until it becomes empty.
elements.sent-2411-118: When this happens, we say that the set $\tilde B_1$ is cleared.
elements.sent-2411-119: Then, we pick another fixed subset $\tilde B_2\subseteq B^{(t)}$ to update.
elements.sent-2411-120: This process is repeated until the epoch is cleared, i.e., $B^{(t) = \emptyset$.}
elements.sent-2411-121: Fix an epoch and let $t_0$ be its first iteration.
elements.sent-2411-122: Let $\tilde{\mathcal B = (\tilde B_1, \tilde B_2, \dots, \tilde B_\ell)$ be a sequence of sets from $B^{(t_0)}$.}
elements.sent-2411-123: In every iteration $t\geq t_0$, suppose that we set \begin{equation}\label{eq:core_sequence_update} g^{(t)}_j := \begin{cases} \alpha x^{(t-1)}_j, \text{ if }j\in \tilde B_{k(t)}\cap B^{(t)} 0, \text{ otherwise,} \end{cases}\end{equation} where $k(t)$ denotes the smallest index such that $\tilde B_k\cap B^{(t) \neq \emptyset$, and $\alpha$ is chosen such that $\|Ag^{(t)}\|_\infty = 1$.}

elements.sent-2411-314: In this section, we give a high-level overview on finding good core-sequences, i.e., having short length and small constituent sets, for the Cut Covering LP.

elements.sent-2411-317: Let $\lambda$ be a lower bound on the value of a min cut and $\varepsilon>0$.
elements.sent-2411-318: A spanning tree $T$ is said to be cleared if every cut in the graph which intersects at most 2 edges of $T$ has weight at least $(1+\varepsilon)\lambda$.
elements.sent-2411-319: Hence, it suffices to find a good core-sequence for $T$, where every set consists of cuts which intersect $T$ in at most 2 edges.

elements.algorithm-2411-006: \caption{$FocusTree_{G,\varepsilon,\lambda}(T,\mathcal{D}_T,w,\congestion)$} \label{alg:tree} \SetKwInOut{Input}{Input} \SetKwInOut{Output}{Output} \SetKwComment{Comment}{$\triangleright$\ }{} \SetKw{And}{and} \SetKw{Or}{or} \Output{A pair $(w,\congestion)$} $B_1\gets \{\text{1-respecting cuts of $T$ with weight in }R\}$\; $\mathcal{D}_T.FastFocus_{G,T,\lambda,\varepsilon}(B_1)$\; Root $T$ at an arbitrary vertex $r\in V$\; Decompose $T$ into paths $\mathcal{P}$ which satisfy \Cref{ppt:bough}\; $FocusMultiplePaths_{G,T,\lambda,\varepsilon}(\mathcal{P},\mathcal{D}_T,w,\congestion)$\; Compute all interested path pairs $(P,Q)$ along with $E_{P\to Q}, E_{Q\to P}, E_{P\downarrow Q}$ \; $B_2\gets \emptyset$\; \ForEach(\Comment*[f]{in parallel}){cross-interested path pair $(P,Q)$}{ Let $P'$ be the path minor of $T$ obtained by contracting edges not in $E_{P\to Q}\cup E_{Q\to P}$\; Let $p'$ be the vertex separating $E_{P\to Q}$ and $E_{Q\to P}$ in $P'$\; $S \gets ExtractCutsInPath_{G,T,\lambda,\varepsilon}(P',p',\mathcal{D}_T)$\; $B_2\gets B_2\cup S$ } \ForEach(\Comment*[f]{in parallel}){down-interested path pair $(P,Q)$}{ Let $P'$ be the path minor of $T$ obtained by contracting edges not in $E_{P\downarrow Q}\cup E(Q)$\; Let $p'$ be the vertex separating $E_{P\downarrow Q}$ and $E(Q)$ in $P'$\; $S \gets ExtractCutsInPath_{G,T,\lambda,\varepsilon}(P',p',\mathcal{D}_T)$\; $B_2\gets B_2\cup S$ } $\mathcal{D}_T.FastFocus_{G,T,\lambda,\varepsilon}(B_2)$\; \Return{$(w,\congestion)$.}

elements.sent-2411-686: Given an undirected graph $G = (V,E)$ with edge costs $c\in \R^E_{\geq 0}$ and an integer $k\geq 1$, the $k$-edge-connected spanning subgraph ($k$-ECSS) problem asks to find a subgraph $H\subseteq G$ which is $k$-edge-connected, spans $V$ and minimizes $c(E(H)):= \sum_{e\in E(H) c_e$.}
elements.sent-2411-687: The natural LP relaxation is given by \begin{equation}\label{sys:kECSS_LP} \begin{aligned} \min \;\; \langle c, y\rangle \text {s.t.} \; \sum_{e \in \delta(S)} y_e \geq k \quad\; \forall\, \emptyset \subsetneq S \subsetneq V \;\; 0\leq y_e \leq 1 \qquad \forall\, e \in E. \end{aligned}\end{equation} This is not a covering LP due to the packing constraints $y\leq \1$.

elements.sent-2411-688: However, they can be replaced with Knapsack Covering (KC) constraints~\cite{CarrFLP00 .}

elements.sent-2411-1201: Given an undirected graph $G = (V,E)$ with nonnegative edge costs $c\in \R^m_{\geq 0}$ and an integer $k\geq 1$, the $k$-edge-connected spanning subgraph (\kECSS) problem asks to find a subgraph $H\subseteq G$ which is $k$-edge-connected, spans $V$ and minimizes $c(H):= \sum_{e\in E(H) c_e$.}
elements.sent-2411-1202: The natural LP relaxation is given by \begin{equation}\label{sys:kECSS_LP} \begin{aligned} \min \;\; c^\top y \text {s.t.} \; \sum_{e \in \delta(S)} y_e \geq k \quad\; \forall\, \emptyset \subsetneq S \subsetneq V \;\; 0\leq y_e \leq 1 \qquad \forall\, e \in E. \end{aligned}\end{equation} This is not a covering LP due to the packing constraints $y\leq \1$.

elements.sent-2411-1324: It suffices to show that \begin{equation}\label{eq:A_delta_x_e_geq_A_g_e} (A(\delta x))_e \geq \frac{\varepsilon}{2}\end{equation} Indeed, assuming \Cref{eq:A_delta_x_e_geq_A_g_e , we show $\delta > \varepsilon/2\eta$ from \begin{equation}(Ax)_e < \eta \Rightarrow (A(\delta x))_e < \delta\cdot \eta \overset{(\ref{eq:A_delta_x_e_geq_A_g_e})}{\Rightarrow} \frac{\varepsilon}{2} < \delta \cdot \eta.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-1326: For any cut $S$, let $seq_{S}$ be the longest sequence, indexed by $j$, of free cuts $\{(S,F_j)\}_j$ that involved in the update procedure so far.
elements.sent-2411-1327: Let $z(S)$ be the last index of the sequence $seq_{S}$.
elements.sent-2411-1328: Let $k_S(j) = k_w(S,F_j)$ where $w$ is the weight at the iteration that $(S,F_j)$ was updated.
elements.sent-2411-1329: Let $k^*_S = k_S(z(S))$ and $seq^*_S := seq_{S (z(S)).$ Observe that\footnote{The summation over an empty set is defined to be zero.. \begin{equation}\label{eq:gseq_eq_sum_xseq} \forall S,g_{seq^*_S} = \delta \cdot \sum_{j=1}^{z(S)} x_{seq_S(j)}.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-1330: The second inequality follows since for every cut $S$, if $e \in \delta(S)$ then $e \not \in F_j$ for all $\{(S,F_j)\}_j$ in the sequence $seq_S$, which follows because of the monotonicity of the weights and the fact that the heavy edges in truncated weights corresponds to free edges in the update procedure.
